req.title: Bulk data export
req.statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-016 specifies a FHIR bulk export capability that must be authorized, queue jobs, and stream NDJSON paginated output. Test design applies EP (authorized vs. unauthorized partitions), BVA (payload size boundaries), NEG (403 unauthorized), and STC (job state transitions). Tests are deterministic, idempotent, and traceable to the requirement's actors, triggers, and error pathways.

tests.name: test_bulk_export_authorized_client_queues_job
preconditions: client holds valid OAuth2 bearer token with bulk:export scope
test database contains ≥1 FHIR Patient resource
actions: POST to /$export with Authorization header (valid token)
poll Content-Location until job status is 'completed'
expected: export stream contains valid NDJSON records
trace: R-HLT-016: actor=user, trigger=client requests export, outcome=export job queued
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from time import sleep

def test_bulk_export_authorized_client_queues_job():
    base_url = '<target_app_base_url>'
    headers = {
        'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_bulk_export_token>',
        'Content-Type': 'application/fhir+json'
    }
    payload = {
        'resourceType': 'Patient',
        'outputFormat': 'application/fhir+ndjson'
    }
    
    resp = requests.post(f'{base_url}/$export', json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f'Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}'
    assert 'Content-Location' in resp.headers
    content_location = resp.headers['Content-Location']
    
    for _ in range(30):
        poll_resp = requests.get(content_location, headers=headers)
        if poll_resp.status_code == 200:
            data = poll_resp.json()
            assert data.get('status') == 'completed'
            assert 'output' in data
            break
        sleep(0.5)
    
    export_resp = requests.get(data['output'][0]['url'], headers=headers)
    assert export_resp.status_code == 200
    for line in export_resp.text.strip().split('\n'):
        obj = json.loads(line)
        assert obj.get('resourceType') == 'Patient'

tests.name: test_bulk_export_unauthorized_client_returns_403
preconditions: client does not hold valid bulk export authorization
client submits request with invalid/expired token or no Authorization header
actions: POST to /$export without Authorization header OR with expired/invalid token
expected: error body contains security/authorization violation message

tests.name: test_bulk_export_large_dataset_paginated_ndjson
preconditions: client holds valid OAuth2 bearer token with bulk:export scope
test database seeded with exactly 10,000 FHIR Patient resources (boundary: large dataset)
export system configured with page size = 1,000 records
actions: POST to /$export requesting export of 10,000 Patient records
retrieve Content-Location and poll until completed
fetch all paginated NDJSON output URLs
deserialize and count all records across pages
expected: output array contains exactly 10 paginated NDJSON files (10,000 ÷ 1,000)
each page ≤1,000 records and is valid NDJSON
total record count across all pages = 10,000
trace: R-HLT-016: large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output
R-HLT-016: boundary case: dataset size = system page threshold × 10
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from time import sleep
from collections import defaultdict

def test_bulk_export_large_dataset_paginated_ndjson():
    base_url = '<target_app_base_url>'
    headers = {
        'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_bulk_export_token>',
        'Content-Type': 'application/fhir+json'
    }
    payload = {
        'resourceType': 'Patient',
        'outputFormat': 'application/fhir+ndjson'
    }
    
    resp = requests.post(f'{base_url}/$export', json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    content_location = resp.headers['Content-Location']
    
    for _ in range(60):
        poll_resp = requests.get(content_location, headers=headers)
        if poll_resp.status_code == 200:
            data = poll_resp.json()
            assert data.get('status') == 'completed'
            break
        sleep(1)
    
    assert len(data['output']) == 10, f'Expected 10 pages, got {len(data["output"])}'
    seen_ids = defaultdict(int)
    total_records = 0
    
    for output_ref in data['output']:
        page_resp = requests.get(output_ref['url'], headers=headers)
        assert page_resp.status_code == 200
        lines = page_resp.text.strip().split('\n')
        assert len(lines) <= 1000
        for line in lines:
            obj = json.loads(line)
            seen_ids[obj['id']] += 1
            total_records += 1
    
    assert total_records == 10000
    assert all(v == 1 for v in seen_ids.values()), 'Duplicate records found'

tests.name: test_bulk_export_empty_dataset_returns_valid_structure
preconditions: client holds valid OAuth2 bearer token
test database contains zero Patient resources (boundary: empty dataset)
actions: inspect output array and any returned NDJSON
expected: output array is empty array [] OR contains no records
no error returned for empty result set
trace: R-HLT-016: boundary case: dataset size = 0 (empty partition)

tests.name: test_bulk_export_malformed_request_returns_400
preconditions: client submits request with malformed JSON or missing required fields
actions: POST to /$export with invalid JSON (missing closing brace)
POST to /$export with unsupported outputFormat value
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request for each malformed request
trace: R-HLT-016: negative path - invalid input handling
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_bulk_export_malformed_request_returns_400():
    base_url = '<target_app_base_url>'
    headers = {
        'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_bulk_export_token>',
        'Content-Type': 'application/fhir+json'
    }
    
    resp1 = requests.post(f'{base_url}/$export', data='{"resourceType": "Patient"', headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 400
    
    resp2 = requests.post(f'{base_url}/$export', data='', headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code in [400, 415]
    
    bad_format_payload = {
        'resourceType': 'Patient',
        'outputFormat': 'application/xml'
    }
    resp3 = requests.post(f'{base_url}/$export', json=bad_format_payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp3.status_code == 400
